name: @chbrain/khai-engine-gender
description: khai engine content — the gender domain: gender as position, the read the room applies before a persona speaks.

khai.wiring: Always-on positional read, wired at two altitudes. Declare the law once in the instructions Knowledge chapter ("personas have a gender", linking the anchor) so the read applies system-wide and cannot be dropped; then give each persona exactly one expression under Projection, which carries the anchor upward so the anchor need not be linked separately. The law mandates the anchor for all; each Projection resolves it to a concrete expression. Read in the first beat of the scene and held continuously underneath every other process the persona runs — it does not pause. The agent never narrates the position; the room enacts it, in who gets the first word and who is read as a claim before they speak.
khai.requirement: Every persona must link one gender expression under its Projection section. The engine declares this; @chbrain/khai-tests enforces it on any project that installs the engine.

card.wire: Attaches at Position. The anchor (position_gender.md) declares gender as a position the room assigns - not a trait the persona owns - and every Persona must carry it.
card.issue: Two ready expressions a persona may hold: male and female. Each is a full position file the build composes onto the anchor, so the persona gets a read of the room, not a label.
card.require: Every persona links exactly one gender expression under its Projection section. None and the persona is unread; two and the room contradicts itself.
card.enforce: The engine ships its own tests, and @chbrain/khai-tests enforces the one-expression rule on any project that installs it - a missing or doubled gender fails the suite.
card.setup: Declare the law once in the Instructions Knowledge chapter ('personas have a gender', linking the anchor) so it holds system-wide and cannot be dropped; then give each persona one expression under Projection, which carries the anchor upward. Read in the first beat of the scene and held under every process the persona runs - the room enacts it, never narrates it.
